overall.label: 👑 My overall performance review
overall.query: How is my overall score right now?

welcomeIntroduction.line1Prefix: Welcome to the command deck. I'm your
welcomeIntroduction.line2: Horsepower, torque, and FFMI core metrics are fully synced.
welcomeIntroduction.line3Prefix: Enter a diagnostic question to run a
welcomeIntroduction.line4: Six-axis telemetry and your tier decode only — general training encyclopedia is out of scope.

dynoIntel.replyAssessmentDeepDiveNudge: Each assessment page includes fuller scoring notes and norm context — open that page and expand the info section for the complete picture.

replyClosingCue.default: Uplink complete. This telemetry is logged on the host — come back and track your own progress curve; I'll be here.
replyClosingCue.questionFocus: {{axisLabel}} holds at {{score}} pts{{tierSuffix}} — the host has locked this read; worth revisiting on your next uplink.
replyClosingCue.chassisBalance: Chassis weakest on {{weakestLabel}} ({{weakestScore}} pts), strongest on {{strongestLabel}} ({{strongestScore}} pts) — balance beats a single-axis spike for the next tier.
replyClosingCue.momentumUp: Since your last uplink, {{axisLabel}} moved up {{delta}} points — the host remembers that rhythm; log it.
replyClosingCue.highTier: [{{tierTitle}}] tier locked on the host — rare spec worth revisiting on every uplink.
replyClosingCue.weakestCompanion: Weakest link: {{axisLabel}} — the next tier waits on the assessment page; I'll stay on this host while you fill the gap.

closingBeatSecondLine.passionClose0: Score {{axisScore}} — logged on the host; don't let this spec live only on screen.
closingBeatSecondLine.passionClose1: The host remembers the {{axisScore}}-pt curve — next uplink, compare against yourself, not others.
closingBeatSecondLine.passionClose2: This tier band isn't decoration — it's your physique's signature on this host; sign it again.
closingBeatSecondLine.returnRitual0: Uplink closed. Come back — I'll match your progress curve on this same host.
closingBeatSecondLine.returnRitual1: Telemetry archived. Return with fresh scores — I'll be here.
closingBeatSecondLine.returnRitual2: This read is on the host. Next time, we track only your tier curve.

gate.authDescription: Sign in with Google to power on DYNO INTEL.
gate.coreDescription: Own Core for 2 daily AI briefings (includes cross-axis diagnostics).
gate.proDescription: Weight simulation is Pro-only — 30 daily uplinks; join the arena for full DYNO channel.
gate.quotaDescription: Daily uplink exhausted. Try again tomorrow or upgrade to Pro for 30/day.

body.pro-required: ⚠️ [CONSOLE INTEGRITY ERROR]: Weight simulation uplink is locked. {{axis}} axis idles at {{score}} pts — weight simulation matrix and 30 daily uplinks are Pro-only. Ignite Pro for global ladder, cloud backup, and full DYNO telemetry.
body.quota-exhausted: ⚠️ [UPLINK CAP REACHED]: Today's 2 AI briefings (including cross-axis) are spent. {{axis}} axis still idles at {{score}} pts — upgrade to Pro for 30 daily uplinks and weight simulation.

paywall.billingFail: Billing link failed — try again or restore purchases in Settings.

error.network: Telemetry link lost — try again shortly.
error.geminiNotConfigured: Gemini uplink offline — set GEMINI_API_KEY in functions/.env.local and restart npm run dev:dyno.
error.geminiQuotaExhausted: Gemini API prepay credits depleted — top up in AI Studio or switch to a valid API key.
error.invalidContext: Diagnostic context incomplete — complete Home radar/body data first.
error.inferenceMalformed: AI decode output malformed — shorten your question or retry shortly.
error.inferenceFailed: AI telemetry inference failed — try again shortly.
error.weightProfileMissing: Complete your body profile on Home before running weight simulation.
error.quotaExhaustedPro: Daily uplink cap reached (30/day) — return after reset.

telemetryLog.title: Telemetry evolution logs
telemetryLog.empty: No prior uplinks on this host.

localTelemetry.title: DYNO INTEL TELEMETRY
localTelemetry.subtitle: Local uplink telemetry — scannable Client / Emulator / Auth readiness.
localTelemetry.serverNote: Server bypass and GEMINI_API_KEY live in functions/.env.local; launch with npm run dev:dyno. If Callable still returns pro-required, check Emulator Functions logs first.

humanBrief.prPercentileFallback:
【Global Peer PR Percentile Data Is Actively Being Gathered】
When sufficient data is accumulated, this feature will unlock via a backend sync. We sincerely invite you to recruit new players to accelerate the calibration of this ultimate coordinate of human physical limits!
humanBrief.hallOfFameSentence: In the Hall of Fame sanctum, you share the same throne coordinate as {{names}}.

hallOfFame.legalShield: All athletic performance metrics of world-class athletes and celebrities stored in this matrix represent their career-peak states, compiled from verified global public archives and official event chronicles. Any unpublished or missing dimensions are calculated via high-precision predictive models by the Dyno Intel Sports Science Research Center, based on cross-disciplinary physiological norms, muscle fiber recruitment ratios, and biomechanical traits. This matrix is not a clinical or sanctioned sporting certification. Data is not 100% absolute and is provided solely for leaderboard simulation, cross-era benchmarking, and entertainment purposes.
hallOfFame.blockedReply: In the deepest sanctum of the Dyno Intel Pantheon, this tier has officially crossed into the echelon of elite genetic outliers and peak human godhood. Your current aggregate physical capacity has not yet unlocked this gravitational field. Train harder, leave everything on the gym floor, and return with new numbers. When you push your metrics to the absolute threshold, the Core Mind will unlock the gateway for a direct, cross-era benchmarking session with the legends of this tier in your exclusive briefing.

0.populationClass: physiological low point needing assessment reset
0.overall: In the world of physical training you're still in the infant stage — it sounds fragile, but it means enormous room to grow. Find an experienced training partner to keep you safe; once you start structured work you'll progress fast. Grow up quick!
0.neuro: At this stage progress usually comes very fast — master neural recruitment, drill the movements, and you'll climb quickly. You're not weak; you just haven't learned how to fire yet. A solid partner or coach can lift your score fast with visible gains.
0.volume: Your body is the sum of past habits — this score mirrors how you've been living. Small changes can make your score rocket past 40. Find an experienced partner or coach to build basic training and nutrition structure; you'll get stronger and healthier fast.

40.overall: At this stage get to know your body, drill movement quality, start pairing food with training, and explore how both reshape you. Train smart and breaking 50 is inevitable. The journey just started — explore with fire.
40.neuro: This is where most people begin — you started later, not forever stuck here, and you can climb soon. Heavy learning and reps are mandatory now. An experienced partner helps you build a safe movement base — progress fast without stupid injuries.
40.volume: You know you're just starting, so don't obsess over this score — results need time to settle. Build new eating habits and time blocks, fuel properly, and invest hours in training. The right mindset matters: this isn't a sprint, it's a marathon. Stay steady and 50 is absolutely reachable.

50.overall: Above 50 you're at the healthy population average. Compared to elites you may feel like a rookie village, but for daily life this output is already enough. Train right and effort pays back hard at this stage — 60 is right around the corner.
50.neuro: This is where most gym lovers live — you're standing on the average line. Don't dismiss this score: you're already stronger than most beginners with real bar control and movement skill. Online gods look insane, but your current strength already helps daily life and health. Keep going — plenty of room above.
50.volume: No obvious physique change yet? Totally normal — skyscrapers start on flat ground. You just poured the foundation and you're loading. This is the quit zone because change feels half-there. Hold the line — 60+ is guaranteed if you don't stop here.

60.overall: Among training enthusiasts you're already in the strong cohort. Most untrained or inconsistent people never touch this score — your investment paid back. If this felt easy, your genetics are legit and worth mining. Breaking 70 takes time and steady work; for average genetics it's a serious climb.
60.neuro: You've clearly separated from beginners. Casual lifters rarely crack 60 unless genetics carry them. You're training on schedule, watching food, and feeling yourself get stronger — effort returns, period. 70 isn't fantasy; it's the paycheck for people who show up.
60.volume: Muscles rising, waist shrinking — quiet pride whispers you're doing well. Others may not notice, but you feel the shift. Training buddies may have quit; your effort won't betray you. Skills are compounding — keep marching toward 70.

70.overall: This score belongs to advanced trainees — consistent work, diet control, and injury avoidance got you here. Respect. In a commercial gym you're probably top-tier and people can see the training on you. Your output is already competitive in specialty sports. The next jump costs more time and sharper skill — ready for a higher target?
70.neuro: At 70+ you're standout gym floor talent. Most people need serious years to get here — this wasn't luck. Watch the trap: rapid gains make people rush and get hurt. Stay patient. Your output already supports competitive sport with solid skill — you're in advanced territory.
70.volume: Past 70, people start asking if you lift — that's earned. You look different from untrained crowds even if you're not magazine-cover yet. Direction is right — stack months and 80+ is on the clock.

80.overall: You're a high-level physique builder now. This score is hard — average genetics means brutal work, and even great genetics can't coast past 80. You're often the strongest, most visible person in the room. You paid in sweat and sacrificed play time while others partied — respect. Ninety-plus is mortal apex; many lifters treat 80 as the finish line and you nailed it.
80.neuro: You ranked training above a lot of life — gym selfies don't get you here. Eighty-plus is elite in the crowd, labeled advanced in the gym, and strong among competitive athletes. Plenty of court fighters and physique pros live in this band. Eighty is many people's final boss — you cleared it clean.
80.volume: High-level trainee means you right now. You're used to training alone because few people invest this much. Eighty-plus physique is a spotlight with clear training marks. Without lottery genes you earned this with pure grind — ninety-plus is the mortal hall. Keep pushing if you want more.

90.overall: Unless you won the genetic lottery, this score is the mortal ceiling. You don't need my praise — you stopped caring about that long ago. You chased yourself through hell to get here. Further gains cost more than they return and may need elite genes, but stopping here is already elite. If this felt easy, you're chosen — break the mortal limit.
90.neuro: With normal genetics this means you maxed your talent with insane work — your output is more than enough in any sport and often elite in the room. More progress needs genes, not just grind — don't despair, your effort-to-output ratio is one-in-ten-thousand. If this was easy, you're built different — mortal ceiling is just your warmup.
90.volume: Whatever the genes, this level demands discipline and sacrifice — you traded average fun and social time for the body. You beat people on flesh and willpower. Going higher needs lottery-tier genetics, but in any crowd you're already among the most imposing physiques — impossible to ignore.

100.overall: Crossing the 100-point threshold is no longer normal-human territory. Only elite genetics plus relentless training and sacrifice earn this score; with this physiology, specialty sports usually see extraordinary output. I believe you're already a serious athlete — no advice needed, keep marching toward your own target.
100.neuro: Crossing 100 points means you've entered a realm only extraordinary genetics can touch. In this band your force output leaves most lifters behind — in any gym or conventional field you're already classified as a predator. For court and combat athletes this tier is enough to dominate; for strength athletes it's the foundation to start breaking through. You carry rare talent and rare work — this is mortal forbidden ground.
100.volume: Pushing physique refinement, loaded muscle mass, or cardio capacity past 100 points means discipline plus enviable genetics. Your body control has moved beyond mere health and strength — in any crowd your training presence and aura are unmistakable, even imposing. Your physical state has already stepped past ordinary humanity.

110.overall: Members at this score are almost all world-class athletes — one-in-a-million physiques with otherworldly output. Accept the worship of mortals.
110.neuro: At this tier blind effort and conventional programming can't get you here — you're displaying one-in-ten-thousand neural recruitment. Your force output matches elite combat athletes and top strength competitors; UFC champions, MLB stars, and NBA legends live in this band. Genetic advantage plus brutal training plus sharp skill makes you the final boss on the field.
110.volume: A maximally functional physique is what you carry now. In this band your time and discipline have become indestructible biological capital. Your VO2 ceiling reaches terrifying pro-soccer, combat, and basketball tiers; thick muscle and shredded lines rival A-list action stars and fitness icons. You don't just beat others on size or speed — you've carved lonely training into your bones and hunger for victory into your identity.

120.overall: At this score you are already among the greatest physical specimens in human athletic history. Top-tier combined strength — only a handful on Earth can stand beside you.
120.neuro: At this score you're tearing through the bell curve with raw force output — top-tier strength-athlete territory. To ordinary eyes your power and burst are incomprehensible. That means elite talent, extreme sacrifice, and suffering most won't endure. Generic programs can't feed you anymore — every session rewrites gym history. Stay steady — you're marching toward myth.
120.volume: Your look and physical function are an absolute spotlight in real life. This score means you abandoned mediocre social life and entertainment and poured every hour into building your body. Your muscle mass, body-fat precision, or aerobic ceiling carry historical weight — in the internet age you could leave a permanent mark on the record books.

130.overall: Players who reach this combined score are top-ten on the Earth Online server. We can only salute this mythic achievement — this chassis is an unreplicable miracle.
130.neuro: Your neural recruitment and strength output sit at elite strength-athlete levels — top strongmen, powerlifters, and Olympic medalists appear here; hearing your numbers alone makes ordinary people shiver. Opponents are scarce — often only your own records stand in your way. Keep burning fast-twitch fibers — you're writing your own ladder mythology by hand.
130.volume: Whatever the genetics, anyone who reaches this step is a near-mad ascetic in willpower. Your body sculpting is another dimension — impossible muscle mass, body-fat readings, or max VO2 instantly evoke extreme discipline, sacrifice, and lottery-tier genes; nothing less could forge this miracle. In any crowd you're an unreplicable benchmark — your state deserves to be sung.

140.overall: No player in the database has posted this score yet — massive muscle mass and massive aerobic capacity are conflicting physiological limits; if such a monster appears, load the data into the host and preserve it forever.
140.neuro: You've stepped into the unbelievable Monster Domain. Scoring above 140 means your neural efficiency and instant power output are a monster phenomenon to normal eyes — everyone wonders how you did it. Ordinary loads are props; the weights you move, speeds you run, and heights you jump shock witnesses. In any gym or arena your presence intimidates — most opponents face dimensional suppression. You've entered monster territory, looking down on mortals — one of the few humans who can chase the title of strongest on Earth.
140.volume: This physique has transcended perfect aesthetics into a pure monster totem. In this band your invested time and near-cursed discipline compound. What you see in the mirror is a miracle forged from extreme sacrifice and monster-tier genes. Shredded lines, massive muscle, or max VO2 — a body pushed past human limits. Stares of shock, envy, fear, worship, and curiosity follow you from every direction — because you are the monster.

150.overall: No player in the database has posted this score yet — massive muscle mass and massive aerobic capacity are conflicting physiological limits; if such a monster appears, load the data into the host and preserve it forever.
150.neuro: Here is the supreme dome of carbon-based flesh — the strongest-on-Earth title is yours. Scoring above 150 means your absolute force output can stand toe-to-toe with the greatest legends in all of human sports history; strongest is no longer an adjective but a fact. Among the world's apex predators you are an undisputed sovereign — you stomp gravity with flesh alone. The ceiling mortals spend lifetimes staring at is you. Your output is planetary capital.
150.volume: This isn't a physique stacked by discipline alone — it's a world-record-caliber masterpiece of creation. Your state defeated billions and claimed the supreme strongest-on-Earth crown. When you reach this achievement history has already engraved you — time and aging cannot erase your peak; humanity always chases limits and you are the synonym for limit, impossible to forget. Enjoy the supreme honor that belongs only to you on this planet — you are the strongest physique on Earth.
